title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures · Protocol Clinic: Net Campus Datacenter Architecture
summary: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net campus datacenter architecture increment in a physical media and topology bill of design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.
Compare mesh, hybrid, star, hub-and-spoke, point-to-point, and failure effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Campus Datacenter Architecture evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-campus-datacenter-architecture-1t2a15" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Campus Datacenter Architecture into observable performance evidence.
